    "id": "ddx-a317208c",
    "title": "exec: post-merge review agent on-by-default in execute-loop",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "ddx",
    "created_at": "2026-04-15T00:49:48.524036278Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-15T05:57:50.41702859Z",
    "labels": [
      "helix",
      "area:agent",
      "area:exec",
      "kind:feature",
      "gap:review-loop"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-32b3008e",
    "description": "execute-loop currently closes a bead on merge. There is no automated post-merge review step validating the commit against the bead's AC. Real-world practice requires a second-opinion agent (typically a higher-capability tier) to produce APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES.

## Principle

Review is on-by-default with zero flags. The tool picks a tier >= the implementation tier (defaulting to smart). The user overrides only in exceptional cases.

## Default flow

- Implementation agent runs at current tier (see ddx-dc231144 tier-based escalation)
- On successful merge, tool automatically renders a review prompt via 'ddx bead review' (ddx-fdf031c9)
- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier + 1, smart)
- Review result is structured JSON: { verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK, findings: [...] }
- APPROVE → close bead, attach review evidence
- REQUEST_CHANGES → reopen bead via 'ddx bead reopen' (ddx-44084b3e), append review body, let the tier-escalation policy pick the next attempt
- BLOCK → stop escalating, flag for human

## Rare overrides

- --no-review (skip review entirely — e.g., doc-only beads in a tight loop)
- --review-harness / --review-model (explicit reviewer override for debug)
- Per-bead opt-out via a 'review:skip' label on the bead

## Dependencies

- ddx-fdf031c9 (ddx bead review command) provides the review prompt template
- ddx-44084b3e (ddx bead reopen) provides the atomic reopen primitive
- ddx-518b2690 (plain --output text) makes extracting the review verdict trivial
- ddx-5bac3c6b (provider health probe) feeds the reviewer tier selection

## Context

Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:
- qwen3-coder-next merged A1 with real AC gaps (missing round-trip tests, zero Postgres coverage, scope creep)
- Without review, the bead would have closed silently and the gaps would surface only at epic rollup — much later and much more expensive to fix
- Review took 41s + a few minutes of reading. Cheap relative to the real cost of shipping broken code downstream.",
    "acceptance": "- [ ] execute-loop runs post-merge review by default
- [ ] Review tier auto-selected (max(impl_tier+1, smart))
- [ ] Review verdict JSON is stable and documented
- [ ] APPROVE closes bead with review evidence attached
- [ ] REQUEST_CHANGES reopens bead via ddx bead reopen and triggers next tier
- [ ] BLOCK stops escalation and flags for human
- [ ] --no-review opt-out works; 'review:skip' label works
- [ ] Integration test: impossible-AC bead produces REQUEST_CHANGES forever without infinite-looping (bounded by max-tier)",
    "notes": "<operator-feedback 2026-04-15>
Critical gap discovered during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:

When a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the REVIEW FINDINGS must be surfaced to the next execute-bead attempt. Today this fails silently because:

1. Review findings posted via 'ddx bead update --notes' are NOT included in the execute-bead prompt.md. Execute-bead threads only <description> and <acceptance> into the XML payload.

2. Therefore a reopen-via-notes does NOT communicate the review findings to the next agent. The agent reads the original description + AC, sees the existing code already matches the description, and declares 'already_satisfied' — auto-closing the bead.

3. The workaround I had to use was updating the DESCRIPTION directly with the review findings. But then the description is no longer a stable spec — it's a moving target carrying review history.

## Required behavior

When a bead is reopened with a review verdict attached, execute-bead's generated prompt MUST include the most recent REQUEST_CHANGES review findings as a distinct section, e.g.:

  <review-findings iteration="2" reviewer="claude-opus-4-6">
  BLOCKING: UserIdentity field 'subject' should be 'external_id' per ADR-018 §3
  PARTIAL: aud-array test asserts serde_json::Error instead of AuthError::CredentialMalformed
  ...
  </review-findings>

And the execute-bead instructions should read: 'If <review-findings> is present, address each BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Do not declare already_satisfied if any BLOCKING finding is unaddressed.'

## Observed failure mode

Sonnet at iter 2 of axon-43ed769b read the prompt (which had the original description with 'subject' and the unchanged AC), verified all type shapes match, ran tests green, and wrote a no_changes_rationale declaring the work complete. The 'already_satisfied' close path auto-closed the bead. The actual BLOCKING issue (wrong field name) was invisible to the execute-bead layer. I had to manually reopen and rewrite the description with fix-up instructions inline.

## This should be part of the post-review epic scope

Making review findings round-trip through the prompt is half of what 'post-review agent in execute-loop' means. Adding to this bead's scope:

- Review verdict JSON includes a 'findings' array with severity (blocking|partial|nit) and location
- Reopen path persists the findings to the bead
- execute-bead prompt rendering pulls latest review findings and injects them as a distinct XML section
- Execute-bead instructions teach the agent to treat blocking findings as first-class work items
- no_changes / already_satisfied paths check for unaddressed blocking findings before closing
</operator-feedback>",

    "title": "Remove lossy agent_runner mirror from .ddx/config.yaml and close env-var gap in native config path",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "ddx",

    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "phase:build",
      "kind:implementation",
      "area:agent",
      "area:config",
      "area:cli"
    ],
    "description": "Remove DDx's lossy embedded-agent config mirror and close the env-var forwarding gap in the native config path.

## Context

FEAT-006 defines native `.agent/config.yaml` as the authoritative config source for the embedded harness. The current `.ddx/config.yaml` `agent_runner` section is a deprecated mirror that duplicates native runtime config fields. Its precedence semantics are documented in FEAT-006 "Embedded Harness Configuration Ownership".

## Goals

- Remove `AgentRunnerConfig` type and `agent_runner` YAML fields from `.ddx/config.yaml` schema once no project uses it
- Remove `AgentYAMLConfig`, `LLMPresetYAML` mirror types from `cli/internal/agent/runner.go`
- Remove the `AgentConfigLoader` injection hook and `resolveAgentConfig` fallback path from `agent_runner.go`
- Apply `AGENT_*` env vars (`AGENT_MODEL`, `AGENT_PROVIDER`, `AGENT_BASE_URL`, `AGENT_API_KEY`, `AGENT_PRESET`) when resolving from native config, closing the gap documented in `TestConfigPrecedenceEnvVarAppliesInDdxFallbackPath`
- Wire the `agentRunner()` cmd factory to pass a working-directory path to native config resolution instead of loading the `agent_runner` block
- Update `TestConfigPrecedenceEnvVarAppliesInDdxFallbackPath` to verify env vars work in the native path after the fix

## Acceptance Criteria

- `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` pass with no regressions
- `.ddx/config.yaml` no longer accepts or uses `agent_runner` fields
- `AGENT_MODEL` set in the environment overrides the model resolved from native `.agent/config.yaml`
- Existing `.ddx/config.yaml` files with `agent_runner` blocks emit a deprecation warning or are silently ignored (no crash)
- The migration path in FEAT-006 remains valid",
    "acceptance": "agent_runner mirror removed from DDx config schema; AGENT_* env vars apply in native config path; all tests pass"
  },

    "description": "During ddx agent execute-bead and ddx agent execute-loop, write a .ddx/run-state.json file at execution start and remove it on completion. This file records the currently-executing bead ID, attempt ID, harness, start time, and execution worktree path so operators and HELIX can observe what is currently running without polling the bead tracker.

This capability is implied by CONTRACT-001 Section 5 (Always-on runtime metrics and provenance) but is currently absent from the DDx source. The post-execution evidence bundle (.ddx/executions/) captures historical facts, but there is no live-state signal during execution.

## Scope

- On execute-bead start: write .ddx/run-state.json with fields: bead_id, attempt_id, harness, model, started_at (RFC3339), worktree_path
- On execute-bead end (success or failure): remove .ddx/run-state.json (or overwrite with status=idle)
- execute-loop may write N run-state entries or a single active one depending on parallelism model
- File must be written atomically (write tmp + rename) to avoid partial-read races

## Contract reference

    "id": "ddx-a946c744",
    "title": "ddx try: pre-merge production-reachability check protocol + runner",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 0,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-02T20:34:44.62487483Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-03T02:57:53.116219848Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:agent",
      "area:checks",
      "kind:platform",
      "prevention",
      "triage:needs-investigation"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-e34994e2",
    "description": "Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract",
    "acceptance": "1. Protocol spec: .ddx/checks/*.yaml schema documented; Go types in cli/internal/checks/protocol.go match the spec; JSON output schema documented.
2. Loader reads all .ddx/checks/*.yaml at runtime; invalid YAML or unknown fields fail loudly with file:line.
3. Runner invokes the configured command with documented env vars; reads ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json; treats non-zero exit as status=error.
4. ddx try integration: pre_merge hook runs ALL applicable checks (filtered by applies_to) in parallel before merge-back; any status=block or status=error aborts the merge and preserves the worktree per existing iteration-preservation conventions.
5. checks_bypass annotation on the bead skips the named check and records an event with the reason; missing reason = bypass rejected.
6. ddx ac run <bead-id> standalone subcommand runs the same checks against the current worktree state and prints results; exit 1 on any block/error.
7. End-to-end test: a fixture .ddx/checks/dummy-fail.yaml with a script that always returns status=block. Run ddx try on a no-op bead; observe the merge is aborted, worktree preserved, evidence file written, event recorded.
8. End-to-end test: same fixture + checks_bypass annotation on the bead → merge proceeds, bypass event recorded.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ shows the check fixture wrote its result.json and ddx parsed it correctly.",
    "notes": "decomposed into ddx-aee651ec (protocol package + ddx ac CLI) and ddx-3e60fd84 (pre_merge integration + checks_bypass + e2e). ddx-3e60fd84 depends on ddx-aee651ec."
  },
  {
    "id": "ddx-a97b10cd",
    "title": "claude harness: write per-iteration trace into execution bundle's embedded/ dir",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "erik",
    "created_at": "2026-04-15T18:59:11.996858451Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-18T06:17:16.973189205Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "area:agent",
      "area:observability",
      "kind:parity"
    ],
    "description": "The embedded ddx-agent harness honors the per-run SessionLogDir override (agent_runner.go:136-138: `logDir := opts.SessionLogDir; if empty fall back to r.Config.SessionLogDir`) so that execute-bead runs drop their JSONL trace inside the bundle at .ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl.

The claude harness does NOT honor that override. claude_stream.go:327 reads only r.Config.SessionLogDir, so every claude execute-bead run writes its trace into the runner-wide default (`.ddx/agent-logs/`) and the bundle's embedded/ dir stays empty. execute_bead.go:446 already passes SessionLogDir: embeddedStateDir through RunOptions; it just isn't used by the claude path.

Empirical evidence from axon's 2026-04-15 ADR-018 session: 2 runs with `harness: agent` produced 34-51 MB traces in embedded/; 23 runs with `harness: claude` left embedded/ empty while `.ddx/agent-logs/` grew to 162 MB.

Fix: plumb opts.SessionLogDir into runClaudeStreaming (either accept it as a parameter or read it off resolvedOpts) and prefer it over r.Config.SessionLogDir when non-empty. Matches the embedded harness behavior exactly.

Parity matters: users want per-run forensic traces attached to the bundle regardless of which harness ran the bead.",
    "acceptance": "- [ ] claude_stream.go:runClaudeStreaming uses opts.SessionLogDir when non-empty, falling back to r.Config.SessionLogDir (matches agent_runner.go:136-138 pattern)
- [ ] A claude-harness execute-bead run writes agent-<sessionID>.jsonl into the bundle's embedded/ dir, not .ddx/agent-logs/
- [ ] Unit test covers the override precedence for the claude path
- [ ] Regression test: existing claude runs without execute-bead context still write to DefaultLogDir
- [ ] Any other harnesses that emit JSONL traces (gemini, codex, etc.) audited and brought to the same override-respecting behavior"
  },

Teach `ddx init` to manage ignore rules for local DDx runtime artifacts and provider-local configuration/state that should not be committed.

- Cover DDx runtime artifacts like execute-bead worktrees and lock state
- Cover local provider state/config directories that DDx may create or depend on during agent execution and that should remain untracked
- Keep tracked project state intact: do not hide durable files like .ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/config.yaml, or checked-in skill/docs content
- Make the init behavior idempotent and safe for existing .gitignore files
- Add tests covering insertion and non-duplication of managed ignore entries",

    "id": "ddx-a9d130d0",
    "title": "EPIC: bead-lifecycle quality framework — make P7 mechanically true (lint + triage)",
    "status": "in_progress",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "epic",
    "owner": "erik",
    "created_at": "2026-05-04T03:18:36.753195174Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-05T04:13:21.48437718Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:beads",
      "area:agent",
      "kind:platform",
      "reliability",
      "bead-quality",
      "adr:023"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-e34994e2",
    "description": "PROBLEM
Per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 P7 ('bead = sufficient sub-agent prompt'), bead authoring quality is a dominant factor in execution success. Audit-1 (commit 86848d21) covered 20 beads; audit-2 (commit b2eb77d0) covered 108 beads; 88 of 108 scored ≤5 against the rubric. Evidence bead ddx-f339c399 documents one observed no_changes outcome (ddx-29058e2a, 2026-05-04) traced to bead-quality gap.

ROOT CAUSE
Today nothing mechanically prevents authoring a bead that's insufficient as a sub-agent prompt OR catches a non-clean execution outcome before it triggers cooldown. The 9 fail-prone layers in ddx try / ddx work (per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES) are mostly fail-closed; bead authoring quality has no observability or enforcement layer at all.

PROPOSED FRAMEWORK
A SKILL-based bead-lifecycle quality framework, layered onto the existing ddx skill (matching the bead-breakdown/ sub-skill pattern at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md). Three modes within ONE sub-skill: lint (pre-dispatch), triage (post-attempt outcome classification), refine (suggest amendments). ddx try integrates via small Go hooks that compose the existing layer-1 runner library to invoke the skill.

NO new top-level skills. NO new ddx CLI verbs. NO LLM judgment baked into Go code.

CHILD BEADS (4 sequential phases)
1. ADR-023 + FEAT amendments (governance + policy)
2. Sub-skill ships to all 5 paths + curated examples
3. ddx try hook integration (warn-only mode) + OutcomeReason field
4. Migration: close stale dups + retrofit 40 HIGH beads (during warn-only; collects baseline)

After child #4 confirms baseline acceptable false-positive rate, BLOCK mode is enabled via config (no separate bead).

NON-SCOPE
- ddx work plan quality column (defer; informational, not blocking)
- ddx bead refine standalone command (defer; refine mode in skill is invocable via ddx run)
- Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first; package later per FEAT-015 project-local model)
- in_progress-as-eligible bug (separate concern; queue correctness, not bead quality)

GOVERNANCE
- ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md (codex correction: not 03-decide/)
- FEAT-004 amend: bead validation hooks + waiver storage + tracker metadata (FEAT-004:150 already owns bead validation hooks)
- FEAT-010 amend: ddx try / ddx work hook ordering + retry classification + outcome records
- FEAT-011 amend: nested workflow-skill packaging (FEAT-011:180 stale — says DDx ships exactly one skill, but bead-breakdown/ exists)
- FEAT-001 amend ONLY if --force --reason CLI flag added (defer to child #3 if needed)",
    "acceptance": "1. All 4 child beads closed.
2. ADR-023 status = Accepted.
3. FEAT-004, FEAT-010, FEAT-011 amendments committed.
4. Sub-skill .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md exists in all 5 shipped paths (codex: skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/).
5. ddx try hooks fire in WARN-ONLY mode by default; BLOCK mode opt-in via config.
6. Baseline lint score distribution recorded post-migration; threshold for BLOCK mode chosen empirically.
7. Closed via final operator review confirming framework is operational."
  },

    "description": "## Symptom

```jsonl
{"data":{"elapsed_ms":7373, "latency_ms":7373, "turn":1, ...}}
{"data":{"elapsed_ms":7995, "latency_ms":7995, "turn":2, ...}}
{"data":{"elapsed_ms":12054,"latency_ms":12054,"turn":3, ...}}
```

`elapsed_ms` is monotonic across the session (cumulative wall time), so per-call LLM latency is unrecoverable from the log.

## Impact

- Cannot answer "what was the LLM-side latency for turn N?" — required for any performance investigation.
- Cannot diff the agent's local-LLM benchmark numbers against captured traces.
- The lucebox-hub/dflash bench_server.py harness needs real per-call latency to validate against reality.

## Likely root cause

Logging code in `cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go` (or equivalent emit site) is writing the wrong value into `latency_ms`. Probably copy-paste from the elapsed-time field. Per-call latency should be `t_response_received - t_request_sent` for that single call.

## Fix sketch

Capture the per-call latency inside the harness adapter (point where the LLM HTTP call is made) and pass it through to the event emitter. Add a unit test asserting `latency_ms < elapsed_ms` for any non-first event.",
    "acceptance": "1. New traces from a `ddx agent` invocation show `latency_ms != elapsed_ms` for events past turn 1.
2. Sum of `latency_ms` across an entire session is <= `elapsed_ms` of the last event.
3. Existing trace files: documented as legacy in CHANGELOG; analysis tools may need to ignore `latency_ms` for runs older than the fix commit.
4. Unit test added that simulates two consecutive llm.response events and asserts latency_ms reflects per-call duration, not cumulative."

    "description": "## Context

ddx-agent v0.3.14 shipped the SSE comment-frame filter that unblocks omlx. Manual dogfooding (2026-04-18) confirmed a single bead executing via vidar-omlx for 6+ minutes with 17,000+ streaming events before the fix caused the regression this bead is meant to prevent: we have no automated assertion that the omlx path actually stays healthy.

Both fresh-eyes reviews (codex + opus, 2026-04-18) flagged: 'missing bead for v0.3.14 consumer-side smoke test in DDx'. This is that bead.

## Fix

Add an end-to-end smoke test that runs a real ddx work invocation against a recorded-or-live omlx endpoint and asserts the attempt closes (or at least produces a closing-status result: success / already_satisfied / a genuine model-side error) without surfacing 'unexpected end of JSON input' or silent stream termination.

Two implementation options:

1. **Record + replay via virtual harness** (preferred for CI isolation). Capture a full AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 trace of a successful vidar-omlx run, store it as a fixture, replay via a test double that emulates omlx's SSE quirks (keep-alive comment frames, reasoning warmup silence, reasoning_content deltas). Test asserts ddx-agent SDK consumes the full stream, ddx work's execute-bead orchestrator records closing status, and bead state mutates correctly.

2. **Live smoke, opt-in** (developer-machine only). A test file guarded by build tag  that makes a real request to vidar-omlx. Useful for catching provider-side regressions (e.g., omlx server ships a breaking change). Skip in CI.

Do BOTH: option 1 for CI signal, option 2 for manual release verification.

## What this prevents

- Regression: a future ddx-agent bump reintroduces the SSE-comment-frame bug or similar streaming defect. CI fixture test catches it before the bump merges.
- Regression: DDx-side change breaks the omlx dispatch path (e.g., adds a header that trips omlx's parser). CI catches it.
- Silent-success: ddx work completes but actually landed a bogus result_rev because of a subtle SSE-frame-dropping bug. Fixture replay asserts the expected final outcome.

## Coverage target

Minimum: one happy-path fixture (successful bead close) AND one error-path fixture (model returns malformed tool-call; execute-bead records task_failed cleanly without corrupting the bead tracker).

## Files likely touched

- cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go (new) — fixture replay + assertions
- testdata/omlx-wire/ (new) — captured AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE JSONL fixtures
- scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh (new) — helper to regenerate fixtures against a live endpoint

- agent-941e7e42 (AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1) shipped in v0.3.14 — needed to capture full fixtures.",

    "id": "ddx-bbb65768",
    "title": "profile/automation: default = local-first with escalation; fast/smart = cloud-first",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "epic",
    "created_at": "2026-04-18T02:51:49.512908486Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-21T17:03:22.145529072Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "phase:build",
      "kind:design",
      "area:agent",
      "area:routing",
      "area:profiles",
      "goal:throughput-per-dollar",
      "wave:deferred",
      "workstream:agent-upgrade"
    ],
    "description": "## Target UX

The user-facing profile vocabulary (cheap / default / fast / smart) should map to concrete routing policies that take full advantage of local models without blocking on their limits. Specifically:

### default profile (the common case)
Try local models first, escalate to cloud if:
- local model's context window is too small for the bead prompt
- local model lacks a capability the bead requires (tools, structured output, etc.)
- local dispatch fails (provider unreachable, timeout, model-side error)
- bead reopens due to post-run check failure (review-malfunction, land-conflict, etc.)

Escalation target: next tier up in the ladder (standard cloud — e.g. codex gpt-5.4 within subscription). If THAT fails, escalate once more to smart cloud. Then stop and unclaim.

### cheap profile
Local only. Never escalate. Intended for high-volume exploratory work where cost dominates and adequate-quality is sufficient. If the cheap tier can't serve the bead, the bead unclaims and waits.

### fast profile
Cloud-first, skip local entirely. Intended for latency-critical or interactive work where local inference's 9s warmup is unacceptable. Starts at a fast cloud model (e.g. gemini-flash or haiku) and escalates to smart on failure.

### smart profile
Cloud-high-quality from the start. Intended for bead-review, adversarial checks, or beads whose AC demands high-quality reasoning. No escalation — if smart fails, the work fails meaningfully.

## Why this matters (dogfooding signal)

As of 2026-04-18, DDx's default is cheap = qwen/qwen3.6 local (vidar-omlx). With v0.3.14 shipping the SSE-comment-frame filter, the local path actually works end-to-end. The queue can drain mostly on local hardware for pennies per bead, escalating only when the bead genuinely needs cloud quality. That's the throughput-per-dollar goal (goal:throughput-per-dollar label).

## Prereqs (already filed under ddx-2d974641 or sibling beads)

This epic is blocked on the autorouting epic delivering its exit-gate UX — 'declare provider + use canonical ref + autorouting works'. Specifically:

- ddx-8610020e (RouteRequest.Provider field) — foundation
- ddx-0486e601 (fuzzy normalization + per-provider) — lets cheap-tier model refs resolve to provider-native IDs
- ddx-2f5a2284 (catalog/Profile discovery integration) — lets Profile tier overrides retarget to provider-native IDs
- ddx-3c5ba7cc (tier escalation --provider affinity) — the lower half of this epic's 'escalate on failure' semantics
- ddx-4817edfd (capability gating) — prevents routing to a model that can't serve the bead

With those in place, the profile-level policy here is mostly CONFIGURATION + a small amount of scoring-layer logic.

## Scope

### Config schema additions

.ddx/config.yaml agent.routing extends with an explicit per-profile ladder:

    agent:
      routing:
        profile_ladders:
          default: [cheap, standard, smart]      # local first, escalate
          cheap:   [cheap]                        # local only
          fast:    [fast, smart]                  # cloud fast, escalate to smart
          smart:   [smart]                        # cloud high-quality, no escalation
        model_overrides:
          cheap:    qwen/qwen3.6
          fast:     kimi/k2.5
          standard: codex/gpt-5.4                # new: subscription cloud, mid-quality
          smart:    minimax/minimax-m2.7

Current profile_priority field (a flat list) becomes a legacy alias for profile_ladders.default with a deprecation warning.

### Tier-escalation policy (routing.go)

For a given profile P, iterate profile_ladders[P]:
- Evaluate model_overrides[tier] resolves on a viable candidate (with --provider affinity from ddx-3c5ba7cc).
- If yes, try it; on failure (execution_failed / land_conflict / post_run_check_failed), move to next tier.
- Record tier decision + escalation chain in routing event on the bead so telemetry reflects where work actually landed.

### User-facing CLI

- ddx work --profile default — the new primary invocation. Most users run this.
- ddx work --profile cheap / fast / smart — opt-in deviations.
- --max-tier continues to cap the ladder (explicit user override beats config).

### Telemetry + measurement

Every execution event records: requested_profile, requested_tier, resolved_tier, escalation_count, final_tier. Aggregated metrics (FEAT-016 / ddx-4b59b816) report per-profile throughput, per-tier cost, per-bead escalation rate.

## Exit gate

'ddx work --profile default --once --local' against a ready bead on a host with a healthy local provider AND healthy cloud fallback:
- Claims the bead.
- Tries cheap tier (local). If it succeeds, closes bead with tier=cheap recorded.
- If cheap fails for any escalation-triggering reason, automatically retries at standard tier without operator intervention.
- If standard fails, retries at smart.
- Final outcome recorded faithfully (success at whatever tier landed, or exhaustion if all tiers failed).

## Out of scope

- Dynamic profile selection ('auto-pick profile based on bead complexity'). Too much magic; explicit profile choice is fine.
- Cost budgets / circuit breakers. FEAT-014 (token awareness) tracks this separately.
- Per-bead profile overrides (bead-level 'requires-profile: smart'). Could be a future follow-up but out of scope here.

## Metadata

Governing spec: FEAT-006 (agent service) + the autorouting epic ddx-2d974641. This bead is the PROFILE-layer policy that sits on top of the routing fixes from ddx-2d974641. Do not start until ddx-2d974641's Phase-1 children are closed.",
    "acceptance": "1. .ddx/config.yaml schema supports profile_ladders (per-profile ordered tier list) AND the legacy profile_priority is still readable with a deprecation warning.

2. Four shipped profile ladders: default=[cheap,standard,smart], cheap=[cheap], fast=[fast,smart], smart=[smart]. Documented in FEAT-006.

3. Tier escalation in routing.go / execute_bead_orchestrator.go iterates profile_ladders[P] on failure, respecting --provider affinity (ddx-3c5ba7cc) and capability gating (ddx-4817edfd). Escalation triggers: execution_failed, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed, structural_validation_failed. no_changes uses the existing cooldown path.

4. Bead routing event records: requested_profile, requested_tier, resolved_tier, escalation_count, final_tier. A run that escalates cheap -> standard -> success at standard has an event with escalation_count=1, resolved_tier=standard, final_tier=standard.

5. 'ddx work --profile default' drains the queue by trying local first and escalating only when needed. Measured outcome over a test run of 10 representative beads shows at least 50% closing at the cheap tier (target: most work lands locally; escalations are the exception).

6. Regression tests cover each ladder path: success-at-cheap, escalate-cheap-to-standard, escalate-all-tiers-then-exhaustion, --max-tier caps the ladder, --profile cheap never escalates.

7. FEAT-006 Profile Routing section updated with the ladder semantics and the intent table (default/cheap/fast/smart).",

  {
    "id": "ddx-bc6d36e0",
    "title": "cli: add 'ddx work plan' — dry-run picker preview showing exact ordering ddx work would pick",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 0,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-03T20:07:58.299023187Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-03T20:32:54.490420167Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:cli",
      "area:agent",
      "kind:feature",
      "reliability",
      "observability",
      "wave:0"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-e34994e2",
    "description": "PROBLEM
Operators cannot inspect what 'ddx work' would pick without actually picking. When an unexpected bead claims (or an expected bead is skipped), the only diagnostic is the diagnostic events emitted AFTER claim attempt — by then the operator has already lost the chance to intervene. There is no read-only 'show me your decision' surface.

ROOT CAUSE
The picker logic at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1145 (ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate) reads the bead store via Store.ReadyExecution (cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246) and applies filters (per-Run attempted map, label_filter intersection) before returning the first eligible candidate. The picker is invoked only INSIDE the loop — there is no standalone API to query 'what would you pick?'.

The existing 'ddx bead ready' command returns ready beads but does NOT apply the picker's per-Run attempted filter (which is non-deterministic across runs anyway), nor does it visualize the picker's tie-breaking rules (priority asc, then updated_at asc per FIFO).

Bead metadata IS deterministic. Given the bead store + stable filter inputs (label_filter, capabilities), the picker's output for the FIRST eligible bead must be 100% deterministic. There is no reason this shouldn't be inspectable interactively.

NAMING NOTE
Originally proposed as 'ddx queue plan'. Renamed to 'ddx work plan' because 'queue' is not an established concept in the CLI surface; 'work' is (the existing 'ddx work' drain command). This makes the new subcommand a natural sibling: 'ddx work' executes the drain; 'ddx work plan' previews what the drain would do.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new subcommand at cli/cmd/work_plan.go (sibling file to cli/cmd/work.go):

  ddx work plan [--label-filter=<labels>] [--capabilities=<csv>] [--limit=<N>] [--json]

Behavior:
1. Read the bead store via the same Store.ReadyExecution path the worker uses (so filters like cooldown, superseded, execution-eligible apply identically).
2. Apply the same sort the worker applies: priority asc, then updated_at asc within priority.
3. Apply optional --label-filter and --capabilities to mirror worker filtering exactly.
4. SKIP the per-Run attempted map (it's non-deterministic across runs; not a stable picker decision).
5. Output an ordered table: Position | Bead ID | Priority | Updated | Status | Filter-decision | Why
   - 'Why' column explains each bead's pick-or-skip reason: 'next claim', 'eligible (rank N)', 'skipped: in cooldown until <RFC3339>', 'skipped: label_filter mismatch (bead labels=[a,b]; worker filter=[c])', 'skipped: capability mismatch (bead requires=[review]; worker has=[bead-attempt])', 'blocked: dep <id> open', etc.
6. Default --limit=10 (top of queue); --limit=0 = full queue.
7. Support --json for machine-readable output (operator scripts; comparison with actual claim events).

Implementation strategy: extract the picker's filter+sort logic from ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate into a reusable function in cli/internal/agent (or cli/internal/bead) — call it 'PreviewQueue(filters PickerFilters) []QueueEntry'. The worker's nextCandidate then calls PreviewQueue + filters out attempted; the new 'ddx work plan' command calls PreviewQueue directly.

This factoring is itself the key insight: the picker logic SHOULD be a pure function over (bead store, filters) returning an ordered list. The worker's role is just 'call picker, claim first, execute'. Separating preview from claim gives us the dry-run + makes the picker testable in isolation.

NON-SCOPE
- Modifying picker semantics (this is read-only preview)
- Predicting downstream effects (e.g., 'after I claim X, what's next?' — out of scope; operator can re-run plan after each claim)
- Server-side picker integration (rev 5 architecture: there is no server-side picker; this command queries the bead store directly)
- Removing the per-Run attempted map (that's owned by C7 ddx-06eb05d8)
- Making 'ddx work' itself accept --plan as a flag instead (subcommand is clearer scope)

INTERSECTION
- Pairs with 'ddx try <bead-id>' (ddx-f261d2a0): operator runs 'ddx work plan' to see ordering, then 'ddx try <id>' to dispatch deterministically.
- Picker priority bug bead ddx-9d55601f shipped diagnostic events at commit 80f51574 (picker.priority_skip, picker.claim_race). 'ddx work plan' is the COMPLEMENT: events show what HAPPENED; plan shows what WOULD happen.
- C8 (ddx-848069a3) relocates routing preflight; 'ddx work plan' should NOT call routing preflight (it's a 'show me the queue' query, not 'show me what could execute given routing health').",
    "acceptance": "1. cli/cmd/work_plan.go implements 'ddx work plan' as a cobra subcommand of 'ddx work'. Help text describes the relationship to 'ddx work' (preview vs execute) and 'ddx bead ready' (picker-aware vs ready-set).

2. Picker filter+sort logic extracted from cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go nextCandidate into a reusable function PreviewQueue(filters PickerFilters) []QueueEntry. ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate now calls PreviewQueue + skips attempted-map members; behavior unchanged.

3. 'ddx work plan' default output: top 10 ready+eligible beads in picker order, with columns Position, Bead ID, Priority, Updated, Status, Filter-decision, Why.

4. 'ddx work plan --limit=0' returns the full queue including skip reasons for non-eligible beads.

5. 'ddx work plan --label-filter=phase:2,area:agent' filters identically to the worker's label intersection.

6. 'ddx work plan --capabilities=bead-attempt' filters identically to the worker's capability matching (when capabilities are implemented; today this is a no-op pass-through).

7. 'ddx work plan --json' emits an ordered JSON array with the same fields, suitable for piping to jq.

8. WIRED-IN: 'ddx work plan' is registered as a subcommand under 'ddx work' (or as a sibling top-level if cobra doesn't naturally support subcommands of leaf commands — verify the existing 'ddx work' structure first). Verified by 'ddx work plan --help' output AND by 'git grep "planCmd\|NewPlanCommand\|workPlanCmd" cli/cmd/' showing the registration. Worker code path imports and calls the extracted PreviewQueue function — verified by 'git grep PreviewQueue cli/internal/agent/' showing the call site.

9. Tests in cli/cmd/work_plan_test.go AND cli/internal/agent/preview_queue_test.go:
   - TestPreviewQueue_PrioritySort — fixture queue P0+P1+P2 mixed; assert P0 first, FIFO within priority
   - TestPreviewQueue_LabelFilter — fixture queue with mixed labels; assert filter intersection narrows correctly
   - TestPreviewQueue_CooldownSkip — fixture bead with execute-loop-retry-after in future; assert skip reason names the time
   - TestPreviewQueue_DepsBlocked — fixture bead with unclosed dep; assert skip reason names the dep
   - TestPreviewQueue_DeterministicAcrossRuns — call PreviewQueue twice with same inputs; assert byte-identical output (no per-Run state leaking in)
   - TestWorkPlan_CLI_TextOutput — assert default text format matches the documented columns
   - TestWorkPlan_CLI_JSONOutput — assert --json output is valid JSON parseable by jq
   - TestPicker_StillWorks_AfterRefactor — assert ExecuteBeadWorker.nextCandidate still returns the first eligible candidate identically to pre-refactor behavior

10. Quality gates:
   - cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/...
   - lefthook run pre-commit

11. Conventional commit message ending [<this-bead-id>]. Stage cli/cmd/work_plan*.go + cli/internal/agent/preview_queue.go + cli/cmd/work.go (subcommand registration line) + the modified cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (only the nextCandidate extraction). DO NOT use git add -A.

12. Manual verification (operator runs after merge):
   - 'ddx work plan' shows current top-of-queue with priorities P0 first
   - 'ddx work plan --limit=0 --json | jq '.[].id'' lists every bead in determined picker order
   - Comparing 'ddx work plan' output to actual ddx work claim events should show identical bead IDs in identical order",

    "title": "bug(server): post-merge reviewer is hard-coded to m.projectRoot, ignores --project flag",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "bug",
    "created_at": "2026-04-15T06:05:45.894010662Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-15T06:44:11.615388119Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "phase:build",
      "kind:bug",
      "area:agent",
      "area:server"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-a317208c",
    "description": "Discovered 2026-04-15 while resolving land_conflict between ddx-0b9c980a (--project flag) and ddx-a317208c (post-merge reviewer on-by-default).

In cli/internal/server/workers.go runWorker, the reviewer construction uses m.projectRoot:

  reviewer = &agent.DefaultBeadReviewer{
      ProjectRoot: m.projectRoot,
      BeadStore:   bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(m.projectRoot, '.ddx')),
      Runner:      m.buildAgentRunner(m.projectRoot),
      ...
  }

But the rest of runWorker uses the local 'projectRoot' parameter, which respects spec.ProjectRoot from the --project flag. When a worker is dispatched against project B via --project, the implementation runs against B but the reviewer runs against A (m.projectRoot). The reviewer reads B's commits from A's bead store, pollutes A's bead events with reviews of B's beads, and uses A's agent runner config for B's review.

Fix: replace m.projectRoot with projectRoot (the runWorker local variable) in all 4 lines of the reviewer construction.

This was introduced when ddx-a317208c landed in parallel with ddx-0b9c980a. The pattern matches: both touch ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec and both extend runWorker. The reviewer was built against m.projectRoot because at the time --project flag did not exist.",
    "acceptance": "(1) reviewer ProjectRoot, BeadStore, and Runner all use the local 'projectRoot' variable, not m.projectRoot. (2) Test: TestExecuteLoopProjectRootOverride is extended to assert the reviewer is constructed against the spec.ProjectRoot, not m.projectRoot. (3) go test ./internal/server/... -count=1 green."
  },

    "description": "<ddx agent execute-loop injects z-ai/glm-5.1 model override that fails, while ddx agent run --harness agent works fine with qwen3.5-27b via vidar LMStudio host.>

ddx agent execute-loop on eitri consistently fails because the execute-loop → execute-bead path injects --model z-ai/glm-5.1, overriding both the ~/.config/agent/config.yaml default_provider (vidar → qwen3.5-27b) AND the models.yaml catalog. The same agent harness works perfectly with direct ddx agent run.

PROOF:

1. dd agent run --text "Hello" --harness agent --timeout 60s succeeds:
   - model: qwen3.5-27b
   - exit_code: 0
   - duration_ms: ~44s

2. ddx agent execute-loop --once --json --harness agent fails:
   - ps shows spawned process with --model z-ai/glm-5.1
   - result: execution_failed, 502 Bad Gateway

3. ddx agent execute-loop --once --json --harness agent --model qwen3.5-27b also fails:
   - Still gets a 404 from OpenRouter or 502 from Alibaba
   - execute-loop's --model flag doesn't propagate to the spawned execute-bead subprocess the way expected

ROOT CAUSE: execute-bead resolves the model from ddx metadata/routing (profiles, targets, or cached model catalog) rather than from ~/.config/agent/config.yaml or from execute-loop's --model flag. The z-ai/glm-5.1 model appears to come from a stale or incorrect routing data source.

EVIDENCE:
- models.yaml defines code-high profile with agent.openai: gpt-5.4
- ~/.config/agent/config.yaml defines vidar with qwen3.5-27b
- Neither source mentions z-ai/glm-5.1
- The model appears to come from a separate routing layer in execute-bead or execute-loop (possibly a cached catalog entry or the server's worker routing config)

FILES:
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop.go (execute-loop model resolution)
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (execute-bead model resolution)  
- cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go (resolveModel chain)",
    "notes": "P0 routing bug. execute-loop/server path picks catalog 'cheap' tier model (z-ai/glm-5.1) instead of locally configured vidar endpoints."
  },

    "description": "<context-digest>
Review area: consumer-facing execute-bead / execute-loop guidance. Evidence covers the shipped CLI help surfaces, the open docs gaps tracked by ddx-a4195a8e and ddx-7a01ba6c, and the need to teach humans and agents one coherent model: execute-bead is the primitive, execute-loop is the normal queue-driven surface.
</context-digest>
Implement the first complete consumer-facing guidance surface for DDx agent execution.

## Required changes
1. Update CLI help text and any nearby command docs so `ddx agent execute-bead` is described as the single-bead primitive and `ddx agent execute-loop` is described as the queue/supervisor command built on top of it.
2. Add or update user-facing operator docs and CLI reference surfaces so users discover `execute-loop` first for normal queue-driven work, with concrete examples for:
   - `ddx agent execute-loop`
   - `ddx agent execute-loop --once`
   - `ddx agent execute-loop --poll-interval <duration>`
   - `ddx agent execute-bead <bead-id>` for bounded/manual runs
3. Update the ddx-bead skill and any relevant internal skill/operator guidance so agents are told explicitly:
   - planning/document-only beads are valid execution targets
   - execute-loop is the default queue-driven entrypoint
   - execute-bead is the lower-level primitive for targeted/manual execution
4. Document the result/close semantics operators need in practice:
   - which outcomes/statuses close a bead
   - which outcomes preserve and leave the bead open
   - which JSON fields automation should read (`status`, `outcome`, `preserve_ref`, `result_rev`, `session_id`)
5. Make the operator guidance consistent with the current merge model:
   - merge is the default successful outcome
   - explicit required execution documents are the automatic merge gates
   - `--no-merge` is the manual preserve override

## Boundaries
- Do not invent a new bead shape or merge-gate field
- Use the existing execute-bead / execute-loop / execution-document contract
- Keep the user-facing guidance aligned with the actual shipped command behavior and documented result surface",
    "acceptance": "CLI help, operator docs, and ddx-bead skill guidance all teach the same model: execute-bead is the primitive, execute-loop is the normal queue-driven surface, planning/document-only beads are valid execution targets, merge is default unless explicit required execution docs block landing or --no-merge is set, and the documented JSON/result semantics tell operators and automation how close/preserve outcomes behave"
  },

    "id": "ddx-c4231775",
    "title": "epic: simplify DDx run/try/work to power-only agent execution",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 0,
    "issue_type": "epic",
    "created_at": "2026-04-30T03:08:40.254166408Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-30T18:47:49.469135377Z",
    "labels": [
      "feat-006",
      "feat-010",
      "area:agent",
      "area:routing",
      "power-only"
    ],
    "description": "## Goal

Simplify DDx agent execution so DDx does not participate in model/provider/harness routing while still owning bead retry policy.

DDx may:
- estimate work effort;
- request abstract agent power bounds using MinPower and optional MaxPower;
- accept operator passthrough constraints (`--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`) and send them unchanged to the agent;
- inspect the agent's available model/power catalog only to choose abstract MinPower thresholds, including top-model-only retries;
- decide whether a bead attempt succeeded using DDx-owned evidence: commits, merge/preserve result, no-changes rationale, post-run gates, review verdicts, cooldowns, and run metadata;
- retry a bead with higher MinPower when policy permits;
- pass non-routing execution intent to the agent: prompt/workdir, power bounds, effort/reasoning, permissions, timeouts, session/evidence metadata, passthrough constraints, and task facts such as EstimatedPromptTokens or RequiresTools.

The agent owns all routing inside the power bounds and passthrough constraints: harness/provider/model validation, endpoint choice, candidate scoring, health, quota, fallback, compatibility errors, and route errors. The agent reports actual model and actual power used, e.g. `running with qwen 3.6-27b (power 10)`.

## Non-goals

- No legacy compatibility paths for DDx-side ladders, model overrides, profiles, or default-path preflight routing.
- No DDx interpretation of harness/provider/model passthrough values.
- No DDx-owned provider probing/filtering in execution setup.
- No candidate traces in execution evidence from ResolveRoute.
- No cheap/standard/smart profile taxonomy in DDx execution policy.

## Required command layering

1. `ddx run`: one invocation atom. Calls agent Execute exactly once with requested MinPower/MaxPower and optional passthrough constraints. Does not call ResolveRoute.
2. `ddx try <bead>`: one bead attempt. Builds bead prompt/worktree/evidence context, estimates initial or retry MinPower, carries passthrough constraints unchanged, then calls run.
3. `ddx work`: queue drain and retry policy. Picks beads, invokes try, classifies results, and schedules higher-MinPower retries when policy permits. It does not inspect passthrough constraint values.

## Passthrough envelope rule

Harness/provider/model fields must live in one narrow agent-passthrough envelope. DDx may copy the envelope between layers and persist it for audit. DDx must not validate, branch on, fallback, rewrite, widen, or use these fields for queue/retry policy. If retry MinPower changes, the passthrough envelope remains unchanged and the agent resolves compatibility.

If hard pins make requested power bounds unsatisfiable, DDx stops with a typed terminal classification such as `blocked_by_passthrough_constraint` or `agent_power_unsatisfied`, records the agent evidence, and reports operator action required. DDx must not remove pins, choose alternatives, call ResolveRoute, or keep escalating.

## Retry eligibility

DDx may raise MinPower only for classified outcomes where stronger reasoning could plausibly help after a valid attempt started, such as capability-insufficient results, post-run check failures after an attempted change, review-blocked capability failures, or no-changes-after-attempt within the no-progress budget.

DDx must not use power retry for deterministic setup/operator-action failures: dirty worktree, merge or land conflict, missing checkout, invalid bead metadata, unresolved dependencies, config parse errors, missing harness binaries, auth failures, command-not-found/toolchain setup, passthrough exhaustion, or any case that would require inspecting/removing/replacing passthrough pins.

## Guardrails

Allowed raw passthrough touchpoints are CLI parsing, the single AgentPassthrough/AgentExecutionIntent value, Execute request construction, execution-record persistence, and human-facing evidence rendering. Retry/work/queue/catalog-threshold code carries the opaque value and may not read Harness, Provider, or Model fields. A routinglint/package-boundary check must enforce this allowlist.

Catalog/status rendering is observability only. Retry threshold policy may consume abstract catalog power numbers to compute MinPower thresholds. Status/catalog code must not feed concrete model/provider/harness choices into execution.

## Documentation corpus gate

This cleanup is not complete when only FEAT-006/FEAT-010 and the DDx skill are updated. The active documentation corpus must be scrubbed so operators, agents, and future implementation beads do not learn stale routing surfaces from older feature docs, test plans, technical designs, solution designs, or operator guides.

Active governing docs must describe `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work`, MinPower/MaxPower, passthrough-only pins, and status/debug-only ResolveRoute. Remaining references to `ddx agent run`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx agent execute-loop`, quorum/profile flags, or DDx-side routing vocabulary must be either removed from active docs or explicitly allowlisted as historical/deprecation/migration context with a reason. A grep-based docs check must make this enforceable.

## Review findings incorporated

- ddx-da19756a: execution path reinjects informational RouteDecision.
- ddx-d4100b7e: execution request drops DDx-owned power intent and actual-power evidence.
- ddx-1997b22d: ddx work preserves legacy execute-loop routing surface.
- ddx-b6a2417b: FEAT-006/specs still bless DDx-side ladders and overrides.
- ddx-a65fea8a: DDx service adapter performs endpoint/model policy.
- ddx-0f42c934: route-status scope leaks candidate traces into execution evidence.
- ddx-d06ae28c: live symptom where default work routes to cheap/incorrect model.
- ddx-6036e1bc: status/catalog work must remain observability and power-threshold only.
- ddx-8e9a11c4: plan aligned to upstream MinPower/MaxPower contract.
- ddx-52ce6249: public docs/skills use run/try/work instead of legacy agent run/execute surfaces.
- ddx-6232daf5: retry eligibility matrix added.
- ddx-b74d4ac6: passthrough exhaustion stop semantics added.
- ddx-b0d96cb9: mechanical passthrough ownership guardrails required.
- ddx-8a617e91: catalog/status display separated from retry threshold policy.
- ddx-02335e0b: repo-wide active docs corpus scrub is a first-class gate.

## Execution order

1. Align specs/docs and test expectations to the MinPower/MaxPower boundary with passthrough constraints.
2. Scrub the active docs corpus and add the docs grep allowlist/check so stale legacy command/routing guidance cannot remain hidden outside the core feature docs.
3. Pull the upstream agent power-bounds interface release: Execute accepts MinPower/MaxPower and passthrough constraints, responses report actual model/power, and model catalog exposes available powers.
4. Remove ResolveRoute and route preflight from run/try/work execution paths.
5. Pass MinPower/MaxPower and passthrough envelope through ServiceExecuteRequest and persist requested bounds, actual power/model, and requested passthrough in run evidence.
6. Replace work alias/execute-loop surface with run <- try <- work layering.
7. Implement DDx retry power policy using the eligibility matrix and agent catalog power thresholds, without inspecting passthrough values.
8. Remove DDx endpoint/model policy from execution adapter.
9. Rescope route-status/provider UI candidate traces to status/debug only.
10. Add routinglint and regression tests that forbid ResolveRoute/model-policy code in execution paths while allowing passthrough envelope plumbing.",
    "acceptance": "1. FEAT-006, FEAT-010, FEAT-001, DDx skill docs, and routing beads state the boundary: DDx chooses MinPower/MaxPower request bounds and retry escalation; agent owns harness/provider/model routing.
2. The active docs corpus has been scrubbed: active FEAT/TD/SD/test-plan/operator docs use run/try/work and MinPower/MaxPower consistently, and any remaining legacy command/routing references are explicitly allowlisted as historical/deprecation/migration context.
3. A checked-in docs grep allowlist/check fails new unallowlisted references to ddx agent run, ddx agent execute-bead, ddx agent execute-loop, --profile, --quorum, --harnesses, DDx-side ResolveRoute execution, profile ladders, or model overrides in active docs.
4. Normal ddx run/try/work execution paths never call ResolveRoute and never re-inject RouteDecision values into Execute.
5. ServiceExecuteRequest receives MinPower/MaxPower and a passthrough envelope on normal execution paths; run evidence records requested_min_power, requested_max_power, requested_passthrough, actual_model, and actual_power returned by the agent.
6. ddx work is no longer an alias for agent execute-loop; it may accept harness/provider/model only as passthrough envelope fields and exposes no DDx-owned routing semantics for them.
7. DDx retry policy uses named eligible/ineligible DDx attempt outcomes to choose higher MinPower on retry, including a top-model threshold derived from agent catalog power numbers; retry policy does not inspect or mutate passthrough constraint values.
8. Passthrough exhaustion stops with blocked_by_passthrough_constraint or agent_power_unsatisfied and never removes pins, widens pins, calls ResolveRoute, or loops escalation.
9. NewServiceFromWorkDir does not filter endpoint candidates by live /models responses or infer model-class routing policy; status surfaces may probe separately.
10. route-status/providers UI may consume ResolveRoute/Candidates or model/power catalog for observability; execution evidence records only requested power bounds, requested passthrough constraints, task facts, and actual route/cost/power returned by Execute.
11. routinglint or equivalent CI guard fails ResolveRoute, RoutePreflight, BuiltinCatalog profile mapping, endpoint live filtering, model-class routing policy, and raw Harness/Provider/Model reads outside the passthrough allowlist.
12. Historical default-route regression is covered: a clean project does not silently force openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini from DDx-side routing logic.",

    "id": "ddx-c53fb89b",
    "title": "Persist execute-loop cooldown metadata after no-progress attempts",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 0,
    "issue_type": "bug",
    "created_at": "2026-04-11T01:22:28.331182212Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-11T05:13:13.828891447Z",

      "helix",

    "description": "<context-digest>
Follow-up observation from the HELIX alpha run that launched `helix-fef22846` via `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` and returned `status=no_changes`, `detail=agent made no commits`, `base_rev=result_rev=de67981a79a27ab8449bf6c475dda677fb25acfc`.
</context-digest>

`ddx agent execute-loop` reported a no-progress result but did not persist the retry-suppression metadata that `ReadyExecution()` expects, so the same bead remained at the front of the execution-ready queue immediately after the attempt.

## Reproduction
1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` and get a `no_changes` result where `base_rev == result_rev`.
2. Inspect bead `helix-fef22846` in `.ddx/beads.jsonl` or via `ddx bead show --json`.
3. Observe the new `execute-bead` event is present, but `execute-loop-retry-after`, `execute-loop-last-status`, and `execute-loop-last-detail` are absent.
4. Run `ddx bead ready --json --execution` again.
5. Observe `helix-fef22846` is still the first execution-ready candidate instead of being suppressed for the cooldown window.

## Observed gap
`ExecuteBeadWorker.Run()` calls `SetExecutionCooldown()` for no-progress outcomes, but the tracker-backed HELIX run did not retain any of the cooldown fields that `ReadyExecution()` checks.

## Why this matters
Without durable cooldown metadata, `execute-loop` can repeatedly burn agent time and tokens on the same no-op bead in back-to-back scans.",
    "acceptance": "After a `no_changes` execute-loop result where `base_rev == result_rev`, the bead persists `execute-loop-retry-after`, `execute-loop-last-status`, and `execute-loop-last-detail`; `ddx bead ready --json --execution` suppresses the bead until the retry-after time; and a deterministic test covers the tracker-backed path rather than only in-memory worker logic."
  },

  {
    "id": "ddx-c67969d5",
    "title": "execute-loop: reopen-retry must reuse initial worker params (harness, model, profile)",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "bug",
    "created_at": "2026-04-25T23:22:06.953399219Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-26T18:17:11.36109409Z",
    "labels": [
      "ddx",
      "kind:bug",
      "area:agent",
      "area:execute-loop",
      "area:retry"
    ],
    "description": "Re-filed from agent-9465287e in /Users/erik/Projects/agent (closed there as 'tracked upstream'; the agent service is not the failure surface). Originally observed in ddx v0.6.2-alpha1 against agent v0.9.9 during SD-024 work.

## Symptom

Worker spawned with explicit pin:

    ddx work --harness claude

First attempt on a bead routes correctly to claude (e.g. opus-4.7) and produces a result. When the post-merge reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES the bead reopens. The next execute-loop iteration retries the bead but the worker's --harness override is NOT propagated to that retry. Tier-attempt events show:

    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=standard harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found
    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=smart    harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found
    kind:escalation-summary winning_tier=exhausted

Note harness= and model= empty — the retry constructs a service request with Harness="". Smart-routing returns zero candidates at every tier above cheap (cheap suppressed by adaptive min-tier from prior failures). Bead bounces between REQUEST_CHANGES (cycle 1) → execution_failed (cycle 2 with no harness routing) repeatedly.

Concrete instance: ddx-dd7fce7e in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/beads.jsonl shows the pattern at events around 2026-04-25T21:46-21:47Z.

## Principle

**Retries should use the same parameters as the initial work.** A reopened bead's retry must inherit every explicit pin (harness, model, model-ref, profile, provider) that the worker was spawned with. Otherwise the retry is a different request, not a retry — and the work cannot converge.

This is a general invariant, not specific to harness overrides. Any explicit pin the worker was launched with must propagate to every iteration on every bead the worker handles, including post-reopen retries.

## Diagnosis (from agent-9465287e worker analysis)

The agent service surface (CONTRACT-003) is correct. The agent has no tier concept — tier classification and adaptive min-tier floors are ddx-side abstractions layered on top of the agent's per-call routing. Loosening the agent's routing engine to return candidates when the harness is empty would change behavior for every other caller and is not the right lever.

The fix is a few lines of option-propagation in the ddx execute-loop's reopen-retry path: when a bead reopens after REQUEST_CHANGES (or any review-driven reopen), the loop must copy the worker-level explicit pins into the next iteration's ExecuteBeadOptions / service.Execute request, the same way it does on the initial claim.

## In-scope files

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or wherever the retry path constructs its execute-bead invocation)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go if review-driven reopens have a separate path
- Regression test asserting the second service.Execute call carries the same Harness, Model, Profile values as the first when the bead was reopened by a reviewer

## Out of scope

- Agent-side routing engine changes — the surface is correct
- Adaptive min-tier behavior tuning (separate concern)
- Tool-loop detection (separate concern, see agent-1bdb9b60 in agent repo)",
    "acceptance": "1. New regression test cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestExecuteLoopRetryReusesWorkerHarness asserts: spawn execute-loop with --harness claude; bead reaches REQUEST_CHANGES → reopen → next iteration's service.Execute request has Harness="claude" (not empty).
2. Same test covers --model, --profile, and --provider propagation across review-driven reopens.
3. Test covers all reopen sources (review BLOCK, review REQUEST_CHANGES, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed, no_changes if it triggers reopen) — every reopen path must propagate pins.
4. Negative test: worker spawned without --harness; reopen retry continues to use auto-routing (no propagation regression).
5. cd cli && go test ./... -count=1 passes."
  },

    "parent": "ddx-e34994e2",
    "description": "Distinct from quota exhaustion (see ddx-aede917d). Rate-limiting (HTTP 429 with Retry-After header — per-second/per-minute throttle) means 'slow down', not 'I'm out for the period'. Provider stays in rotation; current bead retries after waiting.

Per-bead retry policy:
1. On 429 / rate-limit error from Fizeau (NOT NoViableProviderForNow which is quota): wait the Retry-After interval (capped at 60s default, configurable via --rate-limit-max-wait), retry the SAME bead with the SAME provider
2. If no Retry-After header: exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 15s, 30s, 60s) up to the cap
3. Per-bead total rate-limit wait budget (default 5 min); past that, mark execution_failed with reason 'rate-limited beyond budget'
4. Provider availability state unchanged (stays in rotation; this is per-request throttle, not provider-out)

Should use Fizeau's existing RecordRouteAttempt feedback channel for transparency (records the rate-limit event so the routing engine has signal even though it doesn't change provider availability).",

Retry-After honored when present; exponential backoff fallback when absent. 4. --rate-limit-max-wait flag bounds per-bead total wait (default 5 min). 5. Beyond budget: bead transitions per TD's outcome→state mapping for 'rate-limited beyond budget' — status stays in canonical 6, additional context conveyed via labels/events. 6. RecordRouteAttempt invoked for transparency per RateLimitRetryContract. 7. Tests cover Retry-After-respected and exponential-backoff paths. 8. Quota-exhaustion path is explicitly NOT this bead's concern (per QuotaPauseContract separation in TD). 9. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes after this bead lands.**",

Align the governing docs around one execute-bead evidence contract.

## Goals
- Define the execute-bead prompt template as a DDx-owned rationalizer over bead fields plus resolved governing references, not a bead mutation and not a speculative summary of whole specs
- Define the stable tracked artifact set for each execution attempt: prompt, manifest, result, checks, normalized log, and usage/provider pointers
- Define the split between tracked execution evidence and ignored local scratch/runtime state
- Define the default commit policy for successful and preserved attempts
- Define the rule that all programmatically-added commit message metadata must be rendered from tracked machine-readable files",
    "acceptance": "FEAT-006, FEAT-012, FEAT-014, FEAT-015, and API-001 describe the same contract: execute-bead compiles a deterministic prompt from bead data plus resolved references; each attempt produces tracked machine-readable execution artifacts; ignored runtime scratch is clearly separated from tracked evidence; and commit-message metadata is projected from tracked files rather than ad hoc runtime state"
  },

    "status": "open",
    "priority": 1,

    "parent": "ddx-29058e2a",
    "description": "PROBLEM
GraphQL worker dispatch has the same parallel-struct smell as REST, so fixing only the REST handler would leave another transport able to drop future execute-loop fields.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-75 defines a GraphQL-local request struct and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127-141 manually constructs ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec. It also parses poll_interval separately from REST, bypassing a shared spec lifecycle.

PROPOSED FIX
- Decode rawArgs directly into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
- Preserve workers.default_spec behavior from cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:83-104 by applying defaults onto the unified spec before server-side ApplyDefaults/Validate.
- Route GraphQL through the same server-side helper used by REST so ApplyDefaults, Validate, SpecVersion handling, ProjectRoot assignment, and StartExecuteLoop are not reimplemented.
- Delete the GraphQL-local request struct and the parallel StartExecuteLoop literal.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change GraphQL schema or worker kind names.
- Do not change workers.max_count semantics.
- Do not change REST handler behavior; that belongs to the REST child.",
    "acceptance": "1. cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go no longer contains a GraphQL-local execute-loop request struct or a manual ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal.
2. rawArgs decoded directly into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
3. workers.default_spec applied onto the unified spec before server-side ApplyDefaults/Validate.
4. GraphQL routes through the same server-side helper as REST for ApplyDefaults, Validate, SpecVersion handling, ProjectRoot assignment, and StartExecuteLoop.
5. TestGraphQL_WorkerDispatch_UsesExecuteLoopSpec verifies all ExecuteLoopSpec fields survive GraphQL dispatch.
6. TestGraphQL_WorkerDispatch_DefaultSpec verifies workers.default_spec fields are applied before validation.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

Define epic-scoped execution and merge behavior across DDx queueing, git, server, and UI surfaces.

- Define single-ticket-first queue ordering with open epics excluded from the ordinary execute-loop worker
- Define one persistent branch/worktree per active epic and sequential child execution inside that worktree

- Define epic-level merge gates and regular merge-commit landing semantics

| agent-f237e07b| SSE comment-frame filter              | landed v0.3.14  | v0.3.13→14   | b59ebacf    | ddx-bbb2d177 smoke test landed in 1cd8e3a3 and was deleted in d5ebab2b during the v0.5.0 cutover (live-smoke equivalent now lives in the ddx-agent comparison/benchmark suite).   {
    "id": "ddx-d1dae2dd",
    "title": "agent: invoke pre-dispatch lint hook before claim",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 0,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "erik",
    "created_at": "2026-05-04T15:36:23.762184512Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-04T23:48:52.424712225Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:agent",
      "kind:feature",
      "reliability",
      "bead-quality",
      "adr:023"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-e1a576a7",
    "description": "PROBLEM
  ddx try must invoke bead-quality lint after selecting a candidate and before claiming it, but the execute-bead loop currently has no lint hook invocation or warn/block policy at that point.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:640-714 runs PreClaimHook and RoutePreflight before Store.Claim. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:668-710 is the current pre-claim RoutePreflight gate; cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:714 calls Store.Claim. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:29 now exposes ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreDispatchLintHook from closed contract bead ddx-c8dc1146, but no loop path invokes it and no path emits bead-quality.lint events.

PROPOSED FIX
  - In cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go, invoke runtime.PreDispatchLintHook(ctx, candidate.ID) after RoutePreflight succeeds and before Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee).
  - Default behavior is WARN-ONLY: append a bead event kind bead-quality.lint containing score, rationale, suggested_fixes, waivers_applied, and any warning details, then continue to claim regardless of score.
  - Add opt-in BLOCK behavior from resolved config key bead-quality.lint.block_threshold_score. Only a valid hook response with Score below threshold refuses dispatch; infrastructure errors fail open.

  - Defining hook/result types; closed in ddx-c8dc1146.
  - Implementing the runner-backed lint hook body; covered by ddx-68706ef9.

    "description": "<context-digest>
Review area: execute-loop queue-drain reliability after the no-change misclassification fix. Evidence covers repeated installed-binary `execute-loop --once` runs selecting the same execution-ready bead, the new `no_changes` status, the need to auto-close already-satisfied beads without reintroducing false-success closure, and the requirement to avoid infinite retry loops on unresolved no-progress work.
</context-digest>
Improve execute-loop so repeated no-progress attempts are classified and handled in a way that lets the queue make forward progress without sacrificing correctness.

## Goals
- Distinguish already-satisfied no-change beads from unresolved no-progress attempts
- Prevent immediate re-execution loops on beads that repeatedly return `no_changes`
- Keep closure semantics machine-owned and evidence-backed rather than inferred from freeform agent output
- Preserve queue ordering semantics while making the queue drain behavior operationally useful",
    "acceptance": "The queue contains a concrete contract-and-build lane for execute-loop no-change disposition, satisfaction-based auto-close, and retry suppression so queue burning does not stall on one unresolved no-op bead"
  },

  {
    "id": "ddx-d3a4f89c",
    "title": "layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?",
    "status": "closed",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "owner": "erik",
    "created_at": "2026-04-29T22:00:58.431696222Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-29T23:26:16.710773437Z",
    "labels": [
      "area:bead",
      "area:agent",
      "kind:design"
    ],
    "description": "During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?

Three alternatives:

(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.

(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the execute-loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.

(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.

Today's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.

Additionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-<parent>-<timestamp>-<random>-<hex> (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.

Scope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads."
  },

    "status": "open",

    "parent": "ddx-29058e2a",
    "description": "PROBLEM
The REST worker start endpoint drops execution fields because it decodes into a handler-local request struct that is narrower than the CLI/runtime spec.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 defines a private request struct missing opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power, from_rev, and spec_version. cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 then manually constructs ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, creating another silent omission point.

PROPOSED FIX
- Decode POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop directly into executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec.
- Resolve ProjectRoot using existing resolveRequestedProject behavior, write it back to the spec, then call ApplyDefaults and Validate before StartExecuteLoop.
- Add a shared server-side start helper if needed so REST and GraphQL use the same ApplyDefaults -> Validate -> StartExecuteLoop lifecycle.
- Return HTTP 400 for unsupported future SpecVersion with a JSON capability list; keep unknown JSON fields tolerated for newer-server/older-client compatibility.
- Preserve existing 403 trust check and 422 unknown project behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not rework HTTP transport or endpoint path.
- Do not modify GraphQL dispatch; a separate child owns that parallel path.
- Do not change worker runtime consumption beyond what is necessary to pass the unified spec to StartExecuteLoop.",

4. Existing 403 trust check and 422 unknown project behavior preserved.
5. TestRESTWorkerStart_DecodeIntoExecuteLoopSpec verifies all ExecuteLoopSpec fields survive the POST decode path.
6. TestRESTWorkerStart_UnknownSpecVersion returns 400 with capability list.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",
