    "description": "Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:201
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 5349a98 removes the per-harness cutoff and now suppresses legacy sessions.jsonl rows only when usageMirrorKey finds a matching native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id (aggregateUsageAggs lines 201-207 and aggregateUsageFromRoutingMetrics lines 246-270). Current external-harness writes do not guarantee any of those identifiers: both the session row and routing outcome copy result.AgentSessionID, then only fill correlation IDs when present (cli/internal/agent/runner.go:722-753, cli/internal/agent/routing_metrics.go:231-256). For normal codex/claude-style runs without correlation metadata, both records remain unkeyed, so ddx agent usage now counts the same live run twice. Reproduced with the shipped CLI by creating one codex sessions.jsonl row and one matching routing-outcomes.jsonl row with identical tokens/cost but no mirror IDs; ddx agent usage --since 2026-04-01 --format json reported sessions=2, input_tokens=400, output_tokens=40, cost_usd=5 instead of one session.
Suggested fix: Preserve a compatibility boundary for unkeyed current rows, or emit a stable mirror identifier for every routing outcome/session pair before relying solely on identity-match dedupe; add regression coverage for a mixed current-format session/outcome pair that lacks native_session_id, trace_id, and span_id.",
    "acceptance": "A regression test covers a routing-outcomes.jsonl row plus its mirrored sessions.jsonl row for the same harness with no native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id, and ddx agent usage reports one session rather than double-counting; cd cli && go test ./cmd -run TestAgentUsage -count=1 passes."

Pass 1 correctness: API-001 duplicates post-run and land/preserve ownership around Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor
  ddx agent log

Usage:
  ddx agent [flags]
  ddx agent [command]

Available Commands:
  benchmark    Run a benchmark suite comparing agent harnesses
  capabilities Show agent model and reasoning-level capabilities
  condense     Filter raw agent output to progress-relevant lines
  doctor       Check agent harness health
  list         List available agent harnesses
  log          Show agent session history
  replay       Replay a bead with a different agent harness or model
  run          Invoke an agent with a prompt
  usage        Show per-harness token and cost summary

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for agent

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Use "ddx agent [command] --help" for more information about a command., and it validates candidate readiness before resolving the governing base snapshot that FEAT-006 says must control execution-document evaluation.

Implement provider-native adapters that feed DDx routing and reporting.

## Scope
- Codex: native session JSONL adapter for current quota/headroom and recent usage totals

File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:69
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The revised contract now says the supervisor captures the result state reported by `ddx agent execute-bead` and classifies outcomes such as execution failure, post-run check failure, land conflict, and success. The current command surface does not publish that enum anywhere: `ExecuteBeadResult` in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:18-36` exposes only `outcome` (`merged|preserved|no-changes|error`) plus a free-form `reason`, and FEAT-006 documents only landed/preserved iteration outcomes in the commit summary. A supervisor built from API-001 would have to reverse-engineer undocumented meanings from ad hoc strings, which is too brittle for a contract-level dependency.
Suggested fix: Add a documented, stable execute-bead result schema that includes the supervisor-facing state categories API-001 needs, or narrow API-001 so it only relies on the result fields that `execute-bead` actually guarantees today.",
    "acceptance": "The execute-bead contract explicitly documents the supervisor-visible result fields and state enum, and API-001 only depends on that documented surface rather than inferred reason strings"
  },

Pass 1 correctness: the patch fixes hx-1f1e2472's same-harness undercount path and the updated unit tests pass.
Pass 2 integration: aggregateUsageFromRoutingMetrics now relies entirely on mirror-key identity matches when merging sessions.jsonl with routing-outcomes.jsonl. External harness writes do not guarantee native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id, so a current session row and its mirrored routing outcome can both remain unkeyed and get counted twice. Reproduced through the shipped CLI with one codex sessions.jsonl row plus one matching routing-outcomes.jsonl row lacking all mirror IDs; ddx agent usage --since 2026-04-01 --format json reported sessions=2, input_tokens=400, output_tokens=40, cost_usd=5 for that single run.
Pass 3 concern-aware quality: implementation and tests stay within the Go/testing stack and introduce no new attack surface, but the compatibility merge now violates the intended no-double-count guarantee for current runs without correlation metadata.
Pass 4 operational learnings: no AGENTS.md update required; no separate learnings bead required.

File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:59
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: API-001 says the supervisor should read the ready bead set and choose the best candidate using the generic execution-ready validator. But the same contract says HELIX owns which beads are eligible for the loop, and SD-004 already defines deterministic ready ordering as priority then stable snapshot order. The generic DDx validator can say whether one bead is structurally executable; it does not define queue priority or workflow policy. Leaving candidate choice as 'best' via the validator either invents hidden DDx scheduling policy or makes the first worker's queue behavior undefined.
Suggested fix: Separate queue ordering from readiness validation. Specify whether the supervisor consumes a pre-ordered execution-ready queue (for example SD-004 ordering plus workflow eligibility filters) and then uses the validator only as a gate on the selected candidate.",
    "acceptance": "The contract states an explicit queue-selection rule consistent with SD-004 ready ordering and HELIX eligibility ownership, and the generic execution validator is used only to validate structural executability rather than choose policy priority"
  },

    "parent": "hx-epic-ddx-agent-migration",
    "description": "FEAT-006 step 10 specifies 'land by rebase + fast-forward semantics' but the implementation only attempts `git merge --ff-only`.

## Current behavior
If the target branch has advanced since the base revision, the ff-merge fails and the result is preserved under a hidden ref instead of being rebased and landed.

## Fix required
Before attempting ff-merge, rebase the worktree commits onto the current branch HEAD. If the rebase succeeds, then ff-merge and keep linear history. If the rebase fails because of conflicts, preserve the iteration under the hidden ref, leave the target branch untouched, and report the non-landed result deterministically.",
    "acceptance": "execute-bead rebases the worktree result onto current HEAD before attempting ff-merge; when the rebase succeeds the result lands without a merge commit; when the rebase conflicts the iteration is preserved under the hidden ref and the target branch remains unchanged; and tests cover the case where the target branch advanced during execution",

        "type": "blocks"

    "description": "Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:67
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The updated contract still says the supervisor claims the bead, creates an isolated execution worktree, runs `ddx agent execute-bead` inside that worktree, and then removes the worktree. FEAT-006 defines `ddx agent execute-bead` itself as the workflow that creates the isolated worktree (step 4) and always removes it after preserve/land handling (step 12), and the shipped command implementation already does exactly that in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:261-274`. As written, the first shipped supervisor cannot follow API-001 without layering a second worktree lifecycle around one execute-bead attempt or introducing an undocumented lower-level mode. That leaves the core ownership boundary ambiguous and makes the contract non-executable for the intended wrapper design.
Suggested fix: Make one layer own the worktree lifecycle. Either change API-001 so the supervisor invokes `ddx agent execute-bead` from the project root and only reacts to its result, or introduce and document a lower-level execute-bead primitive that accepts an already-created worktree and update FEAT-006/API-001 together.",
    "acceptance": "API-001, FEAT-006, and the execute-bead command agree on a single owner for execution worktree creation and cleanup; the documented supervisor flow no longer requires nested or duplicated worktree management around one execute-bead attempt"
  },

Registry.Discover() calls exec.LookPath directly instead of using the Runner's injected LookPath function.

## Impact
Tests that inject a custom LookPath cannot fully control Discover() behavior. Any future LookPath customization (caching, PATH filtering) won't apply to discovery.

## Fix required
Add a LookPath field to Registry (or pass it via constructor) so Discover() uses the same abstraction as Runner.",
    "acceptance": "Registry.Discover() uses the same LookPath abstraction as Runner.resolveHarness; a test with injected LookPath controls both routing and discovery results"
  }
]

Today ddx try has hook precedents (PreClaimHook, RoutePreflight) but no LLM-invoking hook + no outcome classification carrier.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:24-38 defines ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with PreClaimHook + RoutePreflight closures; no slot for lint or triage hooks.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:657 RoutePreflight runs between pick and claim — exactly where lint should fire.\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:45 ExecuteBeadResult already has reason + failure_mode; ExecuteBeadReport (loop event payload) does NOT — only status/detail/rationale at execute_bead_loop.go:1317.\n- shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1522 already respects Disrupted bool. Codex amendment: KEEP Disrupted; ADD OutcomeReason beside it (do not replace).\n- ddx try at cli/cmd/try.go:327 wires PreClaimHook today; sibling fields will go through the same Runtime construction.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime additions (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go ~line 30):\n   PreDispatchLintHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)\n   PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)\n   LintResult struct { Score int; Rationale string; SuggestedFixes []string; WaiversApplied []string }\n   TriageResult struct { Classification string; RecommendedAction string; Rationale string; SuggestedAmendments string; SuggestedFollowupBeads []FollowupBead }\n\n2. PreDispatchLintHook fires AFTER nextCandidate, BEFORE Claim (sibling to RoutePreflight at execute_bead_loop.go:662).\n   - Hook implementation in cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go: composes runner library to invoke bead-lifecycle skill in lint mode (per FEAT-001/FEAT-010 layer-1 invocation; NOT subprocess via ddx agent run per codex).\n   - WARN-ONLY behavior (default): record lint result as bead event (kind=bead-quality.lint); proceed with claim regardless of score.\n   - BLOCK mode (opt-in via config flag in .ddx/config.yaml): refuse claim when score \u003c threshold; emit operator-readable explanation + cite bead-lifecycle SKILL.md guidance for fixes.\n   - FAIL-OPEN per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES P1: infrastructure failure (skill missing, harness unavailable, bad JSON, timeout) → emit warning event + proceed. Only valid low-score response blocks (and only in BLOCK mode).\n\n3. PostAttemptTriageHook fires AFTER outcome adjudication, BEFORE cooldown decision (read execute_bead_loop.go ~line 880-1100 for the outcome class chain).\n   - Hook implementation in cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go: composes runner library to invoke bead-lifecycle skill in triage mode with (bead + outcome event + session log excerpt).\n   - Triage classification populates new ExecuteBeadReport.OutcomeReason field (string).\n   - shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1522 extends to also short-circuit when OutcomeReason ∈ {transport, quota, routing, timeout, merge_conflict} (transient classes per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES P6). Disrupted check stays in place beside it.\n   - FAIL-OPEN: triage hook failure → no classification → fall through to existing cooldown logic (today's behavior, no regression).\n\n4. cli/cmd/try.go integration: wire PreDispatchLintHook + PostAttemptTriageHook into ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime construction at try.go:327. Operator can disable via --no-lint / --no-triage flags (defer if not needed; defaults are warn-only so explicit disable is rare).\n\n5. Hook invocation cost: lint runs once per dispatch attempt (cheap sonnet call); triage runs once per non-clean outcome. Both invocations cite the bead-lifecycle skill by name in the prompt; harness auto-discovers from filesystem (no special skill-loading code needed).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- ddx work integration (this bead is ddx try only; ddx work integration is a separate follow-up if desired)\n- BLOCK mode threshold tuning (operator chooses post-baseline; no code change)\n- ddx work plan quality column (deferred per epic non-scope)\n- GraphQL state.OutcomeReason exposure (defer; today's GraphQL maps status only at state_graphql.go:493; if exposure needed, separate bead per codex)\n- --force --reason CLI flag (add only if BLOCK mode rollout reveals need; defer)",
